LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRADY LAW GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ITS RENEWAL AFTER BOTH PARTIES HAVE HAD A REASONABLE OPPORTUNITY TO FILE PLEADING MOTIONS, TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY, AND/OR TO SEEK SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE CLAIMS IN THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE REQUEST IN THE REPLY BRIEF THAT THE COURT DISMISS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AND STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED AS BEYOND THE SCOPE OF THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

ORDER DENYING MOTION FOR SANCTIONS
LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRADY LAW GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. OFF CALENDAR, DUPLICATE ENTRY. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS HASSELL LAW GROUP, INC. AND DAWN L. HASSELL'S MOTION FOR STAY IS GRANTED IN PART. THE ACTION IS TEMPORARILY STAYED PENDING A DETERMINATION BY THE BAR ASSOCIATION OF SAN FRANCISCO WHETHER TO DISMISS THE ARBITRATION DEFENDANTS INITIATED AGAINST MR. CORBELLI. IF BASF DOES NOT DISMISS THE ARBITRATION AND THOSE PROCEEDINGS GO FORWARD, THERE IS A RISK OF INCONSISTENT RULINGS AND DUPLICATIVE AND BURDENSOME PROCEEDINGS; ACCORDINGLY, A BRIEF STAY IS APPROPRIATE. IF BASF DISMISSES THE ARBITRATION, THE STAY SHALL TERMINATE BY ITS TERMS WITHOUT THE NEED FOR A FURTHER ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Continued To Aug-14-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Stay
Off Calendar Apr-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Dewitt, Esq., Bradley Law Group, 415-45-7300, appearing for Plaintiff;
Deborah A. Wolfe, Esq., Wolfe Legal Group, 619-234-3363, appearing for Cross-Defendant Hassell Law Group;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAWN HASSELL, HASSELL LAW GROUP, A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION's For Stay.

Defendants Hassell Law Group, Inc. and Dawn L. Hassell's motion for stay is granted in part. The action is temporarily stayed pending a determination by the Bar Association of San Francisco whether to dismiss the arbitration Defendants initiated against Mr. Corbelli. If BASF does not dismiss the arbitration and those proceedings go forward, there is a risk of inconsistent rulings and duplicative and burdensome proceedings; accordingly, a brief stay is appropriate. If BASF dismisses the arbitration, the stay shall terminate by its terms without the need for a further order.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

PLAINTIFF BRADY LAW GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Sanctions.

off calendar, duplicate entry. =(302/EPS)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-15-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Dewitt, Esq., Bradley Law Group, 415-45-7300, appearing for Plaintiff;
Deborah A. Wolfe, Esq., Wolfe Legal Group, 619-234-3363, appearing for Cross-Defendant Hassell Law Group;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRADY LAW GROUP, INC.'s Motion For Sanctions.

Plaintiff Brady Law Group, Inc.'s motion for sanctions is denied without prejudice to its renewal after both parties have had a reasonable opportunity to file pleading motions, to conduct discovery, and/or to seek summary judgment or summary adjudication as to the claims in the cross-complaint. The request in the reply brief that the Court dismiss the first cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty and strike the prayer for punitive damages is denied as beyond the scope of the relief sought in the notice of motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
